sentence: Apple takes its $14 billion tax fight with the EU to court. Representatives from Apple, Ireland and the European Commission argued in hearings at the EU General Court in Luxembourg on Tuesday.  In August 2016, the EU ordered Apple to pay back 13 billion euros ($14.3 billion) in taxes to Ireland, saying the company had received "illegal" tax benefits over the course of two decades. Apple and Ireland are appealing the EU order. The hearings are expected to last through Wednesday, but a decision likely won't be reached for months.
reasoning: Apple 對 EU 的訴訟可能對 Intel 敏感，尤其是 PC cycle 影響，但整體來說仍處於穩定的資金流核心之下。
